w:r.w:t: June 2026 · Confidential · Claude Code Handoff Document
Companion to: FactoryOS V2.1 PRD (June 2026) & Development Plan v1.0
OTP Verification System + MSG91 WhatsApp Notifications
MSG91 (OTP via SMS + WhatsApp Business API for all transactional notifications)
Phase 0-A (employee creation), Phase 0-D (notifications), Phase 0-H (SA Portal — tenant admin creation)
Dropped entirely. MSG91 consolidates OTP + WhatsApp under a single vendor and API.

OTP (One-Time Password) verification is added to OmniFlow for one specific, commercially critical reason: to ensure every user account is linked to a real, working mobile number before it can be used. This is not a security-theatre exercise. Every verified number feeds directly into MSG91's WhatsApp delivery pipeline. An unverified or incorrect number means a user never receives ticket assignments, checklist reminders, or stage transition alerts on WhatsApp — silently breaking the notification layer for that user.

The OTP system is phone number hygiene infrastructure, not a security feature. It exists to guarantee downstream WhatsApp notification delivery via MSG91. Every design decision flows from this rationale.
1.2 Scope — What Is and Is Not Covered
Ensures correct number in system before any WA messages are sent
Additional verification layer for all users
24 hours flat — all roles, no exceptions
Balances security with usability for MSME factory users
All edits are logged in audit history — sufficient accountability
Single vendor; WhatsApp delivery confirms the WA channel works end-to-end
Consolidation: one API, one dashboard, one vendor relationship
MSG91 covers all use cases; two vendors adds operational overhead with no benefit

Once a user successfully completes OTP verification at login, their session is marked as OTP-verified. For the next 24 hours, they are not challenged for OTP again — password authentication alone is sufficient for subsequent logins from the same browser session.
After 24 hours from otp_verified_at, OTP is required again on the next login attempt
Logging out destroys the session entirely — the 24-hour clock resets on next login
24-hour window is flat for all roles — Admin, Manager, Employee, Store Manager, Super Admin
Window is not configurable per tenant or per role in V2 — a single constant in the config file
Do not make the session window configurable in V2. Flat 24 hours everywhere. Configurability can be added in V3 if requested by clients.
Two fields are added to the existing users table. No existing fields are modified.
Gate for login and WA notifications. Set TRUE only after successful OTP verification at account creation.
Timestamp of when the number was first verified. Audit trail. Never updated after initial verification.

mobile_verified defaults to FALSE. A user with mobile_verified = FALSE cannot complete login under any circumstance, regardless of correct password. This is enforced in the authentication middleware, not in route handlers.
Stores all OTP tokens — both account creation verification tokens and login challenge tokens. Tokens are hashed before storage. Plain-text OTP is never persisted.
Links token to the user account
SHA-256 hash of the 6-digit OTP. Never store plain text.
Values: ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION, LOGIN. Prevents token reuse across contexts.
Set to created_at + 10 minutes at generation time
Set TRUE immediately on successful verification. One-time use enforced.
Incremented on each failed verification attempt. Lockout after 5 failed attempts.
Multi-tenancy standard. Required on all tables.

Tokens expire after 10 minutes. Tokens are single-use (is_used = TRUE after first successful verification). After 5 failed attempts on a single token, the token is invalidated regardless of expiry. A new OTP must be requested.
2.3 Modified Table: user_sessions (or equivalent session store)
One field is added to the session record to track OTP verification timestamp for the trusted session window.
Set when user completes OTP at login. NULL means OTP not yet completed this session. Checked on every authenticated request.
All schema changes must be implemented as a single Alembic migration. The migration must be reversible (downgrade() implemented).

MSG91 is the single vendor for all outbound communication in OmniFlow: OTP delivery (SMS + WhatsApp) and all transactional WhatsApp notifications (ticket assignments, checklist reminders, stage transitions, material request updates, etc.). GupShup is not used.
Add the following to the application .env file and the Render environment variable configuration:
MSG91_SENDER_ID=OMNFLW # 6-character sender ID — register in MSG91 dashboard
OTP_SESSION_VALIDITY_HOURS=24 # Trusted session window — do not change in V2
Create a new service module at app/services/msg91.py. This is the single integration point for all MSG91 calls. No other file should call MSG91 APIs directly.
"""SHA-256 hash of OTP for safe DB storage. Never store plain OTP."""
def verify_otp_hash(plain_otp: str, stored_hash: str) -> bool:
async def send_otp_sms(mobile: str, otp: str) -> bool:
"""Send OTP via SMS. Returns True on success."""
async def send_whatsapp_notification(mobile: str, template_id: str, params: dict) -> bool:
"""Send WhatsApp message via MSG91 using a pre-approved Meta template."""
"""Build WhatsApp template component list from params dict."""
Create app/services/otp_service.py. This module handles all OTP lifecycle operations: generation, storage, sending, and verification.
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
async def generate_and_send_otp(db: Session, user: User, purpose: str) -> bool:
Generate OTP, store hashed token, send via SMS.
Invalidates any existing unused tokens for same user+purpose.
Returns True if SMS sent successfully.
# Invalidate existing unused tokens for this user+purpose
def verify_otp(db: Session, user: User, otp_input: str, purpose: str) -> bool:
Verify submitted OTP against stored hash.
Raises OTPError with reason on failure.
Marks token as used and updates user.mobile_verified on success.
This flow applies in two places: (1) SA Portal creating a new tenant admin, and (2) Admin creating a new employee. Both follow identical OTP logic.
Admin/SA fills account creation form including mobile_number
Form validation: mobile must be 10 digits (India), uniqueness checked across tenant
User record created with mobile_verified = FALSE. Account is inactive for login.
generate_and_send_otp() called with purpose = ACCOUNT_VERIFICATION. OTP sent via MSG91 SMS.
User presented with OTP entry screen
Simple single-field form. Shows mobile number last 4 digits. Resend available after 60 seconds.
verify_otp() called. On success: mobile_verified = TRUE, mobile_verified_at = now().
User is now able to log in. Login page shown with success message.
Error shown with remaining attempts. After 5 failures, token invalidated. New OTP must be requested.
User prompted to request a new OTP. Resend button triggers new generate_and_send_otp().

A user with mobile_verified = FALSE cannot log in under any circumstance. The login middleware checks mobile_verified BEFORE checking the password. This prevents timing attacks that could reveal whether a number is verified.
Modified login flow for all user types (tenant users and Super Admin portal users).
Standard credential check. If invalid: generic error (do not reveal which field is wrong).
If mobile_verified = FALSE: block login, show 'Account not verified' message with option to resend OTP.
If valid session exists with otp_verified_at within 24 hours: skip OTP challenge, log user in directly.
No valid trusted session: generate_and_send_otp() called with purpose = LOGIN. OTP sent via MSG91.
verify_otp() called. On success: session.otp_verified_at = now(). User logged in.
For next 24 hours: logins from this session skip OTP challenge (step 3 catches it).
Session destroyed. otp_verified_at cleared. Next login requires OTP again regardless of 24h window.
Modify the existing authentication middleware (app/middleware/auth.py or equivalent) to add two checks on every protected request:
# app/middleware/auth.py — additions to existing auth check
# NEW CHECK 1: Block unverified users at middleware level
# NEW CHECK 2: Check OTP session validity (24-hour window)
# First login after session creation — OTP not yet done
# Trusted window expired — require OTP again
Submit OTP for account creation verification
SA Portal OTP submission — separate route

All WhatsApp messages sent via MSG91 must use pre-approved Meta templates. Template approval typically takes 24–48 hours. All templates must be submitted and approved before Phase 0-D development is considered complete.

Submit all templates to Meta via the MSG91 dashboard BEFORE building the notification delivery code. Template approval is a hard dependency. Do not build the WA delivery layer and then wait for approvals — submit first, build in parallel.
Your OmniFlow verification code is {{1}}. Valid for 10 minutes. Do not share this code with anyone.
Your OmniFlow login code is {{1}}. Valid for 10 minutes. If you did not request this, contact your admin.
The following templates cover all WhatsApp notifications defined in PRD §18.2. All must be submitted as a batch for Meta approval before Phase 0-D delivery code is built.
Hi {{1}}, a new task has been assigned to you: {{2}}. Priority: {{3}}. Due: {{4}}. Login to OmniFlow to view details.
Hi {{1}}, your checklist '{{2}}' is due in 2 hours. Login to OmniFlow to complete it.
Hi {{1}}, your checklist '{{2}}' is now overdue. Please complete it immediately and inform your manager.
Hi {{1}}, ticket '{{2}}' has been flagged for your attention by {{3}}. Login to OmniFlow to review.
Hi {{1}}, a work order '{{2}}' has moved to stage '{{3}}' and is now assigned to you. Login to OmniFlow to acknowledge.
Hi {{1}}, your material request for {{2}} ({{3}} {{4}}) has been approved. Collect from store.
Hi {{1}}, your material request for {{2}} was not approved. Reason: {{3}}. Contact your store manager for details.
Hi {{1}}, stock alert: {{2}} has dropped to {{3}} {{4}}, below the reorder threshold of {{5}} {{4}}. Please reorder.
Hi {{1}}, ticket '{{2}}' assigned to {{3}} has not been acknowledged for {{4}} hours. Login to OmniFlow to follow up.

Template variable counts must be exact. MSG91 rejects templates where the declared variable count does not match the message body. Count carefully before submitting. Variable names ({{1}}, {{2}} etc.) are positional only — document the meaning in this table.
Mobile number display (last 4 digits masked), 6-digit OTP input, Submit button, Resend OTP link (active after 60s countdown), Attempt counter ('X attempts remaining'), Expiry countdown
Same structure as verify_account.html. Different heading: 'Enter your login code'. Logout link to abandon login.
Same as login_otp.html but within the SA Portal template shell. Separate from tenant-facing pages.
No OTP field here. Login form unchanged. OTP challenge is a separate page (redirect after password verification).
No changes to form fields. Post-submit flow redirects to OTP confirmation message page, not directly to employee list.
Same as create_employee — post-submit shows OTP confirmation message for the new admin user's number.
Mobile number is always masked — show only last 4 digits: 'Code sent to +91 XXXXXX1234'
Resend OTP is disabled for 60 seconds after sending — show a countdown timer
OTP input is a single 6-character text field — do not split into individual digit boxes (adds implementation complexity for negligible UX gain)
Show remaining attempts clearly: '3 attempts remaining' — disappears at full attempts
On max attempts reached: show 'Too many attempts. Request a new code.' with Resend button enabled immediately
On expiry: show 'Code expired. Request a new code.' with Resend button enabled immediately
All error messages are user-friendly — no technical codes or stack traces exposed
OTP pages follow the existing dark-theme CSS design system — no new styles needed beyond the OTP input field
Add one task to Phase 0-A immediately after task 0-A-1:
OTP verification on employee account creation — generate_and_send_otp() on user create, block login until mobile_verified = TRUE
Depends on MSG91 service module (0-D-1b). Build 0-D-1b first.
Replace all GupShup references with MSG91. Add the following tasks at the start of Phase 0-D, before any other notification work:
MSG91 account setup — register sender ID (OMNFLW), configure WhatsApp Business number, connect to Meta Business Manager
This is a vendor admin task, not a code task. Must be complete before any MSG91 API calls can be made.
Create app/services/msg91.py — MSG91 service module with send_otp_sms() and send_whatsapp_notification() functions
Single integration point for all MSG91 calls. All other tasks depend on this.
Create app/services/otp_service.py — OTP lifecycle: generate, store hashed, send, verify, invalidate
Submit all WhatsApp message templates to Meta for approval via MSG91 dashboard (all 11 templates in Section 5)
Submit on Day 1 of Phase 0-D. Approval takes 24–48h. Do not wait to start building — submit and build in parallel.
FCM for in-app push remains. MSG91 handles WhatsApp. These are parallel channels.
Add to Phase 0-H task 0-H-4 (Create new client form):
OTP verification on SA Portal tenant admin creation — same flow as 0-A-1b but triggered from SA portal. SA is shown confirmation that OTP was sent to the admin's number.
Depends on 0-D-0c (OTP service). SA does not enter the OTP — the new admin user does on first login attempt.
SA Portal login OTP challenge — separate OTP flow for Super Admin users using superadmin/auth/login_otp.html
SA portal has its own auth middleware. OTP logic is identical but routes are separate.
The following rules are non-negotiable. They must be implemented exactly as stated. Do not deviate for performance, convenience, or client request.

OTP is hashed with SHA-256 immediately on generation. The plain-text value exists only in memory for the duration of the send_otp_sms() call. It is never written to the database, logs, or any persistent storage.
is_used is set to TRUE immediately on the first successful verification. The token cannot be reused even if it has not expired.
After 5 failed attempts on a single token, the token is invalidated (is_used = TRUE). The user must request a new OTP. This limit is enforced in otp_service.py, not in the route handler.
Tokens expire 10 minutes after generation. Expired tokens return EXPIRED error and cannot be verified regardless of correct OTP input. Server time (UTC) is authoritative.

When a new OTP is requested via resend, all existing unused tokens for that user+purpose are invalidated before generating the new one. No two valid tokens for the same user+purpose can exist simultaneously.
Resend is disabled for 60 seconds after each send. Enforce server-side with a timestamp check, not just a client-side countdown. Return HTTP 429 if resend is requested within 60 seconds.
Login failure messages never reveal which field (username or password) is wrong, nor whether an account exists. OTP errors reveal only the attempt count, not the stored hash or any token metadata.
mobile_verified check and otp_verified_at check happen in authentication middleware, not in individual route handlers. There is no way to reach a protected route without passing both checks.
All OTP events are logged: generated, sent, verified_success, verified_fail, expired, invalidated. Log user_id, timestamp, purpose, and outcome. Never log the OTP value itself.
OTP values are never transmitted in URL parameters or query strings. All OTP submission is via POST request body only.
Admin creates employee with valid mobile number
OTP SMS received on that number within 30 seconds. User record exists with mobile_verified = FALSE.
User submits correct OTP within 10 minutes
mobile_verified = TRUE, mobile_verified_at set. Login page shown.
Attempt count incremented. Error shown with remaining attempts. mobile_verified unchanged.
Token invalidated. Resend prompt shown. Further submissions rejected even with correct OTP.
EXPIRED error. Resend prompt. Correct OTP value rejected.
User attempts to log in before verifying
Login blocked. 'Account not verified' message. Resend OTP option shown.
HTTP 429 returned. Client-side countdown enforced server-side.
New OTP sent. Previous token invalidated. Attempt count resets.
User logs in with correct password, no existing trusted session
OTP challenge page shown. OTP sent to verified mobile number.
session.otp_verified_at set to now(). User logged in. Dashboard shown.
User logs in again within 24 hours (same session)
No OTP challenge. Login proceeds directly to dashboard.
User logs out and logs back in within 24 hours
OTP challenge shown. Logout destroys session regardless of 24h window.
User logs in after 24 hours have elapsed
OTP challenge shown. otp_verified_at was > 24h ago; session trust expired.
User with mobile_verified = FALSE attempts login
Blocked at middleware before password check. 'Account not verified' message.
WhatsApp message received using omniflow_ticket_assigned template within 60 seconds.
No WhatsApp message sent. FCM push only (if FCM implemented). No error thrown — silent skip with audit log entry.
WhatsApp message received using omniflow_checklist_due template at correct time (2h before due).
MSG91 API returns error on send
Error logged. Notification marked as failed in audit log. Application continues — notification failure does not break the triggering action.
MSG91 returns 400. Error logged. Fallback to SMS if SMS template exists. No application crash.
The following files are created or modified as part of this feature. Claude Code should validate this list against the actual codebase before beginning implementation.
MSG91 API integration — OTP SMS + WhatsApp notifications
OTP lifecycle: generate, hash, send, verify, invalidate
OTPToken SQLAlchemy model (or add to existing models file)
OTP route handlers: verify-account, login-otp, resend-otp
Database migration: users fields + otp_tokens table + sessions field
Add mobile_verified check + otp_verified_at trusted session check
Modify login flow to redirect to OTP challenge after password verification
Mirror login flow changes for SA Portal
Trigger OTP send after employee creation
Trigger OTP send after tenant admin creation
Add all MSG91 environment variables from Section 3.2
Add MSG91 config fields to settings class
The following decisions were explicitly made and locked during the product design session. They must not be reversed without a formal plan amendment.
Account creation + every login. Not on edits.
Phone number hygiene for WA delivery. Edits covered by audit history.
Flat 24 hours. All roles. Not configurable in V2.
Balances security with MSME usability. Simplicity preferred over flexibility.
Single vendor for OTP + WA. Better SMB fit, cleaner API, Indian support.
Consolidation. One API, one dashboard, one billing relationship.
SMS primary. WhatsApp secondary (same MSG91 vendor).
WA delivery also validates the WA channel end-to-end.
Ensures correct numbers before any WA messages sent.
SHA-256 hash only. Never plain text.
Balance between usability and security. Standard for transactional OTP.
OmniFlow — OTP & MSG91 Integration · Development Amendment Plan v1.1 · June 2026 · Confidential · Internal Use Only
